applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel with 2D grid + BLOCK_SIZE vectorization
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented Triton kernel for MaxPool1D using 2D grid (batch, feature) with each program processing BLOCK_SIZE=1024 output positions via vectorized load/store operations. Key design: (1) 2D grid for (batch, feature) pairs, (2) Third grid dimension for sequence blocks, (3) Vectorized processing of BLOCK_SIZE positions per program using tl.arange and masks, (4) Proper float32 casting for AMD ROCm, (5) Kernel window iteration with bounds checking via masks.

attempted_but_failed.name: 1D grid with one program per output element
attempted_but_failed.reason: Too many programs (800M+) caused massive launch overhead

attempted_but_failed.name: 1D grid with break statement
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support break statements in kernels
attempted_but_failed.error: UnsupportedLanguageConstruct: unsupported AST node type: Break

attempted_but_failed.name: Incorrect index calculation in 1D flattened grid
attempted_but_failed.reason: Index decoding from flat index was incorrect

top_bottlenecks: Triton kernel launch overhead minimized with block processing

notes: Key insight: For large tensors (64*192*65523 = 803M elements), one-program-per-element approach has prohibitive launch overhead
Solution: Use 2D grid (batch, feature) with third dimension for sequence blocks, each program handles BLOCK_SIZE=1024 positions
Triton on AMD ROCm requires explicit float32 casting for loads/stores
BLOCK_SIZE should be power of 2 and at least wavefront size (64 for AMD CDNA4)
Achieved 3.35x speedup (1.82ms vs 6.09ms baseline) with Score: 83.5
